title: Stelis oblongifolia
taxonomy: Stelis oblongifolia is classified as a species of orchid, more specifically within the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. This subtribe is a part of the large orchid family, Orchidaceae. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is native to the tropical regions. The classification of Stelis oblongifolia is further detailed within the family Orchidaceae, specifically within the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification is based on its unique characteristics, such as its growth habit and flower structure.
uses: Stelis oblongifolia has been used in traditional medicine for its potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Stelis oblongifolia, also known as the "Coral Root" plant, is a type of orchid native to North America, characterized by its delicate white or yellowish flowers and long, narrow leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stelis oblongifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in salves and poultices to treat various skin conditions and wounds.
